data.body:
Kicked off 2026-06-29. Two updates to the ticket framing:

**Target bumped v0.19.0 → v0.20.0.** v0.20.0 (2026-06-26) dropped after this ticket was filed and is now latest. It's a bigger change than the v0.19.0 maintenance release: AWS Terraform provider upgraded to v6 across all root modules (Account/Network/Build-repo/Database/Service/CI-CD), DB-role-manager Lambda off EOL Python 3.9 → 3.14, CloudWatch log retention 1mo → 1yr. No hard code migration for template-provided functionality (just `make infra-update-*` reruns + a one-time `make infra-upgrade-terraform-deps` provider-cache refresh), but the AWS v6 upgrade guide should be reviewed for OSCER-specific resources beyond the template.

**Base layer run on branch `giverm/template-infra-update-v0.20.0`.** `nava-platform infra update .` updated base 0.17.0 → 0.20.0, then stopped at conflicts before the app layer (expected, given the base/app skew). Did NOT auto-commit (phase had conflicts), so all changes are in the working tree.

Result:
- **34 files merged cleanly** — the provider-v6 `.tf` changes across infra/modules, accounts, Makefile. No .tf conflicts.
- **17 conflicts, all small** (1–6 markers each, ~33 total): 14 in `.github/workflows/*`, 3 in `e2e/` packaging.
- **App layer (`update-app --all`) still pending** — run after base conflicts resolved.

Key finding: OSCER is AHEAD of the template on e2e/CI deps (Playwright 1.61.0 vs template 1.56.1, axe-core 4.11.3 vs 4.10.1, etc.) thanks to already-landed Dependabot bumps. The v0.19.0 "Playwright bump" that originally motivated this ticket is moot — template would downgrade. Resolution principle for version conflicts: keep-ours.

No push, no terraform apply. Branch is local/reviewable.
